resolutions.action: Replaced vague citations with specific peer-reviewed studies (Causevic et al. 2011, Madeyski 2010, George & Williams 2004, Nagappan et al. 2008), specific Stack Overflow Developer Survey years with sample sizes and data points, and Boehm & Basili cost-of-defect figures. Added quantitative backing for all major claims.

Rewrote all recommendations to ground each directly in specific cited evidence. Added concrete coverage thresholds, tooling categories, CI/CD integration guidance, measurable success criteria, and team workflow integration points derived from the findings.

Added substantive engagement with the ROI debate in the Introduction, characterizing the opposing arguments (maintenance burden, diminishing returns at high coverage thresholds, context dependency) and explicitly scoping the report's position.

Expanded each finding from a single bullet point into a full subsection with: (a) clear definition, (b) mechanism by which the benefit is produced, (c) empirical or case-study evidence, and (d) acknowledged limitations of the benefit.
Rewrote executive summary to include scope of research conducted, strength of evidence found, the primary recommendation, and significant caveats in 3-5 sentences.

Added a dedicated paragraph addressing the report's own methodological limitations: source selection bias, recency of evidence, generalizability across languages and domains, and absence of a formal systematic review protocol.
Added a Methodology subsection (2.5) describing the research approach: sources/databases consulted, keywords used, inclusion and exclusion criteria, and date range of literature reviewed.
